sanskrit: तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् । क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ॥
hindiTranslation: उन द्वेष करनेवाले, क्रूर स्वभाववाले और संसारमें महान् नीच, अपवित्र मनुष्योंको मैं बारबार आसुरी योनियोंमें गिराता ही रहता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: I cast those hateful, cruel, and vile people again and again into demonic births.
essence: Repeated hatred drives a person into worse and worse forms of existence.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People who hate, act cruelly, and live in impurity fall into lower births again and again.
simpleInsight: Cruelty narrows the future. Repeated hostility keeps a person trapped in worse patterns.
detailedMeaning: Krishna continues his warning to Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. After describing the traits of destructive people, he says that those who hate, harm, and degrade themselves do not rise toward freedom; they sink into worse births and conditions.
modernRelevance: A person keeps choosing contempt, manipulation, and violence, then wonders why life feels smaller each year. The habits do not stay private; they shape the kind of future being built.
translationLiteral: तान् (those) / अहम् (I) / द्विषतः (hateful) / क्रूरान् (cruel) / संसारेषु (in the cycles of worldly existence) / नराधमान् (lowest among men) / क्षिपामि (I cast) / अजस्रम् (again and again) / अशुभान् (inauspicious, impure) / आसुरीषु (into demonic) / एव (indeed) / योनिषु (wombs, births)
insight: It shifts the mind from blaming fate to seeing how repeated cruelty hardens into a downward pattern.
